items.title: Default to OO subplots for anything beyond one cell
items.avoid: Chaining plt.plot / plt.scatter across helper functions without passing Axes.
items.prefer: fig, ax = plt.subplots(...) at the top; pass ax into helpers; return fig from plotting functions.
items.why: Explicit Axes eliminate global-state bugs and simplify testing and savefig.
items.example: def plot_loss(ax, train, val): ax.plot(train, label='train'); ax.plot(val, label='val'); ax.legend()

items.title: Set figsize and dpi before saving for reports
items.avoid: Relying on screen-default size then upscaling a PNG in Word or LaTeX.
items.prefer: fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5)); fig.savefig('fig.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight').
items.why: Vector PDF/SVG or high-dpi raster prevents blurry axis text in publications.
items.example: For paper figures: savefig('loss.pdf', bbox_inches='tight') for scalable text.

items.title: Label every series that shares an axes
items.avoid: Two plt.plot calls with no label= argument.
items.prefer: ax.plot(x, y, label='Train'); ax.plot(x, z, label='Val'); ax.legend().
items.why: Legends are the only way to distinguish overlapping lines in loss curves and metric plots.
items.example: Training monitor: label='train acc' and label='val acc' on the same epoch axis.

items.title: Manage overlap in scatter and hist for large n
items.prefer: alpha=0.3–0.5, smaller markers, or subsample for overview plots.
items.why: Readable density patterns prevent false EDA conclusions from ink saturation.
items.example: ax.scatter(x, y, s=8, alpha=0.35) on a 20k-row tabular subset.
